description:
Goal: make Compiler2's structured body/control model strong enough to represent `case`/`with`/`receive` without falling back to old-world CPS helpers or positional tailness tricks.

Signal:
- Compiler2 no longer has to reject structured control because `LoweredBlock { steps, result }` is too weak a contract
- the body/backend/native model carries explicit destinations/resumes and dispatch-owned arm identity in a way later tickets can consume mechanically

Change:
- redesign the local Compiler2 control representation around explicit control destinations/resume points, taking the useful internal idea from `../hybrid.html` but keeping it compiler2-owned and per-function
- preserve dispatch facts, executable identity, return ABI, and callable-entry inventory
- do not add a new whole-root artifact rung

Why:
- today Compiler2 can lower only straight-line steps plus `If`; `case`/`with`/`receive` want an explicit control contract, not more nested block special cases
title: Compiler2 body/control model: make structured control explicit
